generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) paired with cycloidal gear transmission for joint motion control in MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, providing mechanical amplification of torque with unspecified gear ratio."
]

instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear mechanism integrated with PMSM for torque amplification and speed reduction in robotic joint motion, critical for precision control in manufacturing applications."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary angular displacement sensor mounted directly on motor shaft to measure rotational position for backlash mitigation in gear transmission."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary angular displacement sensor positioned behind gearbox to provide redundant position measurement and improve system backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring joint torque in robotic axis to support friction identification and motion control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current measurement sensor for PMSM to enable electrical parameter monitoring and control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance TorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
]

instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model defined using SysML v1.7 state machines representing control workflows and motion state transitions for robotic joint."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionControlEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model implemented using MontiArc Automaton framework for representing complex control state transitions in robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulinkMultiPhysicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-physics simulation model in MathWorks Simulink integrating mechanical (friction dynamics), electrical (PMSM behavior), and control system components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance AADLArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Architecture description model using AADL defining component-connector interfaces, interconnections, and composition for system engineering."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ArchitectureEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-series sensor data streamed via MQTT protocol containing angular displacement measurements from motor and gearbox encoders."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted, GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ProcessedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted data representing identified joint friction patterns derived from torque/current measurements and position data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueDataTransmitted, CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionCompensationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalMotionTraces : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical data traces containing motion profiles and performance metrics for trend analysis and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted, GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MotionControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based state machine execution engine implementing closed-loop motion control for robotic joint using position feedback."
    DTDFVocab:enables JointMotionControlService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton-based friction identification system processing torque/current data to estimate joint friction characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCompensationService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink co-simulation environment enabling multi-physics validation of control strategies and friction models."
    DTDFVocab:enables VirtualCommissioningService
]

instance ArchitectureEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based architecture management tool maintaining consistency across system layers and enabling cross-layer traceability."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDesignService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data aggregation pipeline processing raw sensor streams into structured time-series data for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance FrictionCompensationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++/Java-based friction compensation algorithm dynamically adjusting control parameters based on real-time friction estimates."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyOptimizationService
]

instance AnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE-integrated analytics platform performing predictive analytics on historical motion data for maintenance forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

// C6: Services
instance JointMotionControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time motion control service implementing closed-loop position control for robotic joint using SysML state machines."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionPrecisionInsight, ControlParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active friction identification and compensation service dynamically adjusting torque profiles to mitigate friction-induced errors."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionPatternInsight, CompensationAlgorithmAction
]

instance VirtualCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offline simulation service enabling testing of motion control strategies in virtual environment before physical deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

instance SystemDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based system engineering service maintaining consistency across requirements, functional, logical, and technical layers."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignConsistencyInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time monitoring service tracking joint health and operational parameters via sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalHealthInsight
]

instance MotionAccuracyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive motion accuracy optimization service continuously refining control parameters based on friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccuracyImprovementInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service analyzing historical motion data to forecast equipment degradation and recommend interventions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationForecastInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MotionPrecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight into joint motion precision derived from position tracking and friction compensation algorithms."
]

instance FrictionPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified friction patterns in cycloidal gear and PMSM system enabling proactive compensation strategies."
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results from virtual commissioning simulations confirming control strategy effectiveness before physical deployment."
]

instance DesignConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of cross-layer consistency between requirements, functional architecture, logical design, and physical implementation."
]

instance OperationalHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of joint health status based on real-time sensor data and friction analysis."
]

instance AccuracyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics showing improvement in motion accuracy through dynamic friction compensation."
]

instance DegradationForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics results forecasting potential equipment degradation based on historical motion patterns."
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of control parameters (torque profiles, velocity trajectories) to maintain motion precision."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CompensationAlgorithmAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of friction compensation algorithms to dynamically adjust motor control inputs based on real-time friction estimates."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment containing MABI MAX 100 robotic system with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, CycloidalGearTransmission,
                  MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder,
                  TorqueSensor, CurrentSensor
]

// C5: Components
instance RobotJointAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical assembly combining PMSM, cycloidal gear transmission, and associated sensors for robotic joint motion."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance ControlSystemInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Interface component connecting physical sensors/actuators to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller for motion command processing."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Software pipeline processing raw sensor data through aggregation, abstraction, and analysis stages for decision support."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAX100RobotEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **real-time, data-driven synchronization** between physical and digital representations, characterized by **live-data linking** between the physical object and its digital counterpart. While the document does not specify quantitative synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms), it implies **temporal alignment** is critical for the digital shadow's operational validity, as it relies on **continuous data aggregation and abstraction** from contextual traces. Behavioral modeling tools like **SysML state machines** and **MathWorks Simulink** are integrated to describe system dynamics, necessitating **temporal granularity** for accurate simulation and predictive analytics. However, explicit latency requirements or service-specific time scales (e.g., control loops, monitoring intervals) are not detailed in the text."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular, component-based architecture** defined by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as SysML IBDs, AADL, or MontiArc, which describe systems via **components, connectors, and composition**. Behavioral orchestration is achieved through **state machines** (e.g., SysML v1.7) and **MontiArc Automaton**, enabling **automated workflows** for data processing and system behavior modeling. The document does not explicitly detail **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., orchestration patterns, resource management, or plug-and-play integration), but it implies **integration of heterogeneous tools** (e.g., Simulink for detailed behavior) to achieve cohesive system operation. The **digital shadow** itself is described as consisting of **several data series**, suggesting a structured aggregation of contextual traces for unified representation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between stages involves **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavioral modeling is further refined using **SysML state machines (v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) integrated with general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance ensures alignment across heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives, leveraging **interoperability frameworks** to unify disparate modeling tools and notations. The lifecycle is iterative, with continuous refinement from abstract requirements to executable technical models, ensuring traceability and consistency across phases.

Digital Shadows, as a precursor to DTs, are characterized by **one-way data aggregation** (contextual data traces) for specific purposes, lacking autonomous operation or feedback mechanisms. Their lifecycle is implicitly embedded within DT development but remains passive, relying on external systems for interpretation and processing.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
Not in Document"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is primarily defined by **MQTT-based communication** for streaming sensor data from physical assets (e.g., a Siemens CNC controller). The document does not specify **network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency constraints**, nor does it mention **reliability mechanisms (e.g., redundancy, failover)** or **security protocols (e.g., TLS, encryption)**. While the broader context implies reliance on **industrial communication protocols**, only **MQTT** is explicitly referenced. The system’s connectivity is constrained to the **one-way data flow** characteristic of a digital shadow, lacking bidirectional or control-oriented interactions.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **software tools** such as **MathWorks Simulink** and other simulation environments for behavior modeling, alongside **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for implementation. Deployment infrastructure is not explicitly described, but the system integrates **visualization dashboards** for monitoring and analysis. The documents do not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms (e.g., containerization, orchestration), or availability/reliability assurances. Instead, the focus is on **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** across modeling notations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **heterogeneous modeling tools and general-purpose languages**, including **SysML**, **MathWorks Simulink**, and **code in C++/Java** (Section II.A), enabling cross-tool interoperability for comprehensive system description. Data exchange is facilitated via **MQTT** (Section IV.A), a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol for streaming real-time data between physical and digital components. Additionally, the abstract mentions **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a standardized interoperability framework (e.g., under **Industry 4.0** paradigms) for integrating Digital Twins with enterprise systems. The system also references **interoperability standards** implicitly through the use of ADLs (e.g., AADL, SysML), which define standardized interfaces and connectors for component-based engineering. However, specific integration patterns with other Digital Twins or detailed dependencies on external systems (e.g., ERP, MES) are not elaborated in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry standards** and **interoperability frameworks** primarily through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key standards include:
- **SysML (Systems Modeling Language, v1.7)** for **functional/logical architecture** and **behavioral modeling** (state machines).
- **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** for **component, connector, and interface specification**.
- **MontiArc** for **model-driven architecture evolution** and **automaton-based behavior**.
- **ACME** for **generic component modeling** in distributed systems.
Compliance frameworks are inferred via **toolchain integration** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, AADL compilers**), ensuring adherence to **ISO/IEC standards** (implicitly referenced in MBSE practices). Interoperability is achieved through **unified modeling notations** (e.g., **SysML IBDs**) and **stakeholder alignment mechanisms**, though explicit **certification requirements** or **formal compliance frameworks** (e.g., **IEC 62264, ISO 15926**) are not detailed. The system prioritizes **heterogeneous tool interoperability** over rigid standardization bodies.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly describe cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, or fail-safe mechanisms for the Digital Twin system. However, in **Document [42]**, the concept of a 'safety cell' is mentioned in the context of robotic automation, indicating a physical safety measure for the MABI robotic arm (referenced in **Document [44]**) to prevent collisions and ensure operator safety. No technical details regarding digital security (e.g., OAuth 2.0, X.509, or AAS security submodels) or safety-critical system constraints (e.g., RAMI 4.0 safety layers) are explicitly outlined in the provided documents. The focus of the referenced materials is primarily on model-driven engineering, digital shadow implementation, and friction modeling for robotics, without addressing cybersecurity or data governance frameworks."
]
